question: Compare Figure 1.2-4 (NPM external, Ch.01) with Figure 5.4-17 (Pressurizer Region, Ch.05) to understand the upper module design.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written text. It lists the relevant diagrams, legends, and tables provided for both electrical/I&C and piping/instrumentation systems, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be supplied by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings are included in the report to visually clarify design information, with legends provided for symbols used in these diagrams. It lists the relevant tables and figures containing I&C functional diagrams, electrical one-line diagrams, and piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams will be provided by applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates at full power using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps. Its small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design features passive safety systems, dedicated emergency cooling, and decay heat removal, with the reactor and containment partially immersed in water for effective heat removal and enhanced safety. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvers using control rods and boron concentration adjustments, and its components are designed for in-shop fabrication and modular assembly.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for core refueling and maintenance, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and inspection.

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building and include spaces for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock, all designed to facilitate safe fuel handling and reactor maintenance operations. These areas provide equipment and access for refueling, fuel assembly transport, and storage, while ensuring radiation protection for personnel.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across different power levels by establishing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures, using both software and manual calculations. It accounts for uncertainties in flow resistance and core bypass, ensuring low pressure losses to support natural circulation, and confirms that certain uncertainties, like SG tube plugging, do not affect primary coolant flow rate determination.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0016.title: Principal Site Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0016.summary: This section provides a general overview of the NuScale plant design, outlining key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains that each Combined License (COL) applicant must reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and supplement it with site-specific information, while distinguishing between certified design elements and conceptual design information (CDI). Security-sensitive details are specially marked and withheld according to regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, noting that some information is representative or conceptual and marked accordingly, while security-related details are withheld per regulations.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures for maintaining reactor vessel integrity in the NuScale Power Plant. It details the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation over a 60-year design life, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies the use of nondestructive examinations, controlled shipping environments, and quality assurance measures, and lists the key codes and standards used as references.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGB), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.
